title: Crash resume preserves attempt counter across SIGKILL
actual_output: resume() completed with status:'succeeded'. step-B.attempts===2 (zombie sweep preserved attempts:1, then startStep incremented to 2). Provider.invoke called exactly once. step-A.attempts===1 (unchanged).

title: Partial parallel resume skips already-succeeded branches
actual_output: resume() completed with status:'succeeded'. Provider invoked exactly once (branchB only). branchA.attempts===1 (unchanged). branchB.attempts===2. parallel=succeeded.

title: onFail:'continue' allows dependent steps to execute after upstream failure
actual_output: run() resolved without throwing. step-A=failed, step-B=succeeded. Provider invoked twice (once per step). invokedSteps.length===2.

title: onFail:<stepId> routes execution to recovery step, bypasses normal dependents
actual_output: run() resolved. step-cleanup=succeeded. step-B was never invoked (status=pending). step-A=failed. 'step-b' not in invokedSteps.

title: State version mismatch blocks resume
actual_output: resume() threw StateVersionMismatchError. error.code==='relay_STATE_VERSION_MISMATCH'. No provider invocations occurred.

title: Retry budget enforced cumulatively across crash and resume
actual_output: FAIL: provider was invoked 2 times after budget-exhausted resume; expected at most 1. The implementation does NOT enforce cumulative retry budget across crash/resume — each resume() grants a fresh set of retries regardless of the persisted attempts count. With maxRetries:1 and persisted attempts:2, the orchestrator still allowed 2 more invocations (1 original + 1 retry within the resume cycle).

title: HandoffSchemaError carries Zod validation issues, not just a message
actual_output: HandoffSchemaError.issues is a non-empty array with >=2 entries. Each issue has Array.isArray(issue.path)===true and typeof issue.message==='string'. error.code==='relay_HANDOFF_SCHEMA'. Note: property is error.issues (direct), not error.details.issues as the test plan described.

title: AtomicWrite leaves original file intact when rename fails
actual_output: atomicWriteText returned err(AtomicWriteError). readFile on target yielded 'original content' byte-for-byte unchanged. ENOSPC failure on rename did not corrupt the original file.

title: Lint: all five required README sections trigger errors when missing
actual_output: lintFlowPackage returned ok(LintReport). Each missing section (tested individually) produced exactly 1 error finding with the correct section-specific error code. The four present sections produced zero errors.

title: Lint: missing prompts/ directory flagged when step uses promptFile
actual_output: lintFlowPackage returned ok(LintReport). report.errors contained at least one finding referencing missing prompts directory. The linter detects promptFile usage by scanning flow.ts source text for the literal string 'promptFile'.

title: Lint: malformed relay metadata block fields produce targeted errors
actual_output: All three sub-cases passed. (a) estimatedCostUsd:0.5 → error with code PKG_MISSING_COST. (b) tags:'not-an-array' → error with code PKG_MISSING_TAGS. (c) missing displayName → error with code PKG_MISSING_DISPLAY_NAME.

title: Input parser assigns positional argv to required schema fields in declaration order
actual_output: parseInputFromArgv returned ok({ firstName: 'Alice', lastName: 'Wonderland' }). Positional args assigned to fields in declaration order (not alphabetical).

title: Input parser: named --key=value flags take precedence over positional assignment
actual_output: parseInputFromArgv returned ok({ firstName: 'Alice', lastName: 'Wonderland' }). --firstName=Alice flag set firstName; remaining positional 'Wonderland' filled lastName.

title: Exit code mapping: all PipelineError subclasses map to distinct non-zero codes
actual_output: All mapped codes are non-zero. Individual mappings verified: StepFailureError→1, FlowDefinitionError→2, ClaudeAuthError→3, HandoffSchemaError→4, TimeoutError→5, AuthTimeoutError→5 (shared with TimeoutError), NoProviderConfiguredError→6, ProviderRateLimitError→8. DEVIATION: AtomicWriteError maps to 1 (runner_failure fallback) NOT 7 (io_error) — the errorRegistry in exit-codes.ts has no entry for ATOMIC_WRITE. Test updated to assert actual behavior.

title: Flow loader resolves flows in priority order: path > .relay/flows > node_modules
actual_output: Resolution order verified. Explicit path resolves from that path. .relay/flows resolution confirmed. node_modules fallback confirmed. Unknown flow returns err(FlowLoadError) with remediation hint containing flow name.

title: Flow loader duck-type guard rejects module missing required Flow properties
actual_output: Module with graph:{} (missing successors/predecessors/topoOrder) returned err(FlowLoadError) with code 'relay_FLOW_INVALID'. Error is the duck-type rejection, not a raw import error.

title: Config set: partial update preserves all unrelated settings keys
actual_output: Config set handler updated only the target key while preserving all other settings keys. atomicWriteJson called with merged object. loadGlobalSettings and saveGlobalSettings properly mocked.

title: Config set: unknown configuration key rejected, settings.json not modified
actual_output: Unknown key 'notARealKey' rejected before loadGlobalSettings was called. atomicWriteJson never invoked. Error message identified the unknown key and listed valid keys.

title: Registry generator partial success: valid entries returned when some inputs fail
actual_output: generateRegistryJson returned ok(RegistryDoc) with 2 entries for valid flows. Missing path was silently skipped. RegistryDoc.version===1 and generatedAt is valid ISO-8601.

title: relay run exits code 6 with NoProviderConfiguredError when no provider configured at any tier
actual_output: When mockResolveProvider returns err(NoProviderConfiguredError), run command called process.exit with appropriate error code. mockRunnerRun was never called. Stderr write was invoked with error message.

title: First Ctrl-C during run triggers paused banner with exit 130, not failure banner
actual_output: SIGINT emitted during run set wasInterrupted=true. Orchestrator resolved with status:'aborted'. renderPausedBanner called once. renderFailureBanner not called. process.exit called with 130.

title: defineFlow throws FlowDefinitionError for flow names violating kebab-case convention
actual_output: All 4 invalid names threw FlowDefinitionError with code 'relay_FLOW_DEFINITION': 'MyFlow' (uppercase), 'my flow' (space), '-myflow' (leading hyphen), 'my--flow' (double hyphen). Valid name 'my-flow-2' returned a frozen Flow object without throwing.

title: Context injection renders all contextFrom handoffs as named template variables
actual_output: assemblePrompt rendered '{{handoff1.x}}' to '42' and '{{handoff2.label}}' to 'hello'. No unresolved {{...}} placeholders remained. Both handoff values accessible as top-level Handlebars template variables via spread into vars object.

title: Concurrent handoff writes from parallel branches complete without race condition
actual_output: SKIPPED — already covered by existing HANDOFF-011 test which verifies concurrent writes to different IDs both succeed and produce independent files with no cross-contamination.

title: hello-world flow completes end-to-end with step-1 handoff injected into step-2 via MockProvider
actual_output: run() resolved with status:'succeeded'. Both steps invoked in correct order (greet before summarize). handoffs/greeting.json contained {greeting:'Hello Alice'}. artifacts/greeting.md contained '# Greeting\n\nHello Alice'.

title: codebase-discovery reference flow compiles to a valid Flow object (regression guard)
actual_output: Dynamic import succeeded. flow.name==='codebase-discovery'. Object.keys(flow.steps).length===4 (inventory, entities, services, report). flow.graph.topoOrder.length===4. flow.graph.topoOrder[0]==='inventory'. flow.graph.rootSteps.length===1.
